what: Supervised-from-scratch baseline on TUAB (same EEGEncoder1D, trained end-to-end with a linear head + class-balanced CE, early-stopped on a patient-disjoint dev split carved from TRAIN). Two held-out-patient numbers per seed: (a) END-TO-END (encoder+head, mean-pooled window logits) and (b) FROZEN-PROBE (freeze the supervised encoder, fit the SAME StandardScaler+balanced-LogReg probe as the SSL eval -> apples-to-apples with our SSL 0.819).

provenance.split: TUAB patient-disjoint 2717 train / 276 eval; dev = 15% of TRAIN patients (dev_seed=0, fixed across model seeds; no eval leakage)
provenance.fairness: same architecture, same TUAB-train data, same augmentations as the SSL views, class-balanced CE; early stopping on dev balanced-acc (patience 8, max 60 epochs)

honest_reading: BALANCED ACCURACY: end-to-end supervised (0.817) TIES our frozen SSL (0.819) -- gap -0.002, within noise. Frozen-probing the supervised encoder is slightly lower and far noisier (0.797 +/- 0.018 vs 0.819 +/- 0.002); the +0.022 SSL edge is ~1.2 std, NOT a clean win.

AUROC: supervised is consistently HIGHER (0.906 e2e / 0.908 frozen vs 0.891 SSL) -- supervised features RANK better; SSL trades ranking for better thresholded balanced-acc and far more stability across seeds.

STABILITY / OVERFIT: SSL frozen std 0.002 vs supervised frozen std 0.018. Seed 1 trained longest (48 epochs) and overfit most (worst held-out 0.773 frozen); seeds that stopped early (13/15 epochs) generalized better. Supervised is seed-sensitive; SSL is not.

TAKEAWAY: NOT 'SSL beats supervised'. The honest claim is PARITY -- a same-architecture model with FULL task labels matches our LABEL-FREE frozen probe on balanced accuracy, more stably; supervised only edges ahead on AUROC. This supports the label-efficiency value prop, not SSL superiority.
